questions.text: Over the past 2 weeks, how often have you felt tired or had very little energy?
How old are you?
What is your biological sex?
questions.help_text: This determines which reference ranges and conditions apply to you.

questions.text: How would you describe your overall health?
What is your height and weight?
questions.help_text: Used to calculate BMI for the health models. Leave blank if unsure.

questions.text: What is your usual blood pressure?
questions.help_text: Enter your most recent reading if you know it. Systolic is the top number, diastolic is the bottom.

questions.text: What is your waist circumference?
questions.help_text: Measure around your waist at the level of your belly button. Leave blank if unsure.

questions.text: What is the highest level of education you have completed?
Were you admitted to a hospital overnight in the past year?
How many prescription medications do you currently take?
questions.help_text: Include pills, patches, injections, and inhalers prescribed by a doctor.

questions.text: How many hours do you usually sleep?
questions.help_text: Count from when you fall asleep to when you wake up.

questions.text: How often do you snore while sleeping?
questions.help_text: Based on what you've been told or noticed yourself.

questions.text: Has a doctor or other health professional ever told you that you have trouble sleeping?
In the past 3 months, what best describes your work schedule?
How many hours did you work in total last week (all jobs combined)?
Does your job involve moderate physical activity?
questions.help_text: For example: walking, carrying light loads, cleaning, or cooking.

questions.text: In your free time

options.label: Moderate activity
options.help_text: Brisk walking, dancing, gardening, light cycling

questions.text: On days when you drink alcohol, how many drinks on average?
questions.help_text: Enter 0 if you don't drink. One drink = one beer, glass of wine, or single shot of spirits.

questions.text: Have you ever had 4 or more drinks (if female) / 5 or more drinks (if male) on almost every day?
Have you smoked at least 100 cigarettes in your entire life?
Do you currently smoke cigarettes?
In the past 30 days, how many cigarettes did you smoke on an average day?
How soon after waking up do you smoke your first cigarette?
Have any of the following ever been confirmed by a doctor?
questions.help_text: Select all that apply — leave blank if none.

questions.text: Are you currently taking prescribed medication for high blood pressure?
Are you currently taking insulin?
Do you take pills or tablets to lower your blood sugar?
Have you actively tried to lose weight in the past year?
Has a close family member (parent, sibling, or child) been diagnosed with diabetes?
How many times did you receive any healthcare in the past year?
questions.help_text: Include doctor visits, urgent care, telehealth, and other appointments.

questions.text: Were you told you had high blood pressure on two or more visits?
Have you had an asthma attack in the past 12 months?
Which type of arthritis were you diagnosed with?
Has a doctor ever advised you to reduce fat or calories in your diet?
Would you like to weigh more, less, or about the same as you do now?
On a typical night, how many times do you wake up to urinate?
How often do you experience any urinary leakage — even just a small amount?

options.label: Every day or almost every day

questions.text: Have you had urgent episodes where you couldn't reach the toilet in time?
How often do you leak urine before reaching the toilet?

options.label: Every day or almost every day

questions.text: Physical symptoms

options.label: Short of breath on stairs or uphill

questions.text: Have you seen a doctor about this abdominal pain?
How much urine do you usually lose each time?
Does urinary leakage happen during physical activities like coughing, sneezing, or exercise?
How much does urinary leakage affect your daily activities?
How often does urinary leakage happen during physical activities?

options.label: Every day or almost every day

questions.text: Are you currently pregnant?
Have you ever been pregnant?
How many times have you been pregnant?
Have your periods been regular in the past year?
questions.help_text: Regular means roughly the same cycle length each month.

questions.text: At what age did you have your last menstrual period?
questions.help_text: Leave blank if your periods are ongoing.

questions.text: Have you ever used female hormones?
questions.help_text: For example: HRT (hormone replacement therapy) or the contraceptive pill.

questions.text: Upload your lab results
questions.help_text: Upload a PDF or photo of your blood test. Values are extracted automatically and included in your report.
